claim_text: Mississippi's comprehensive early literacy package increased grade 4 reading achievement relative to comparison states.
notes: Evaluation source is currently a stable metadata record so effect-size extraction still needs the full article text.

claim_text: Science of Reading should be treated as a broad instructional movement rather than a single intervention with one causal effect estimate.
notes: Use this row to prevent overstated claims in the viewer while anchoring to foundational evidence-synthesis and practice guidance sources.

claim_text: Common Core implementation produced positive initial math effects overall but those gains were uneven across student subgroups.
notes: Bleiberg reports stronger initial gains for economically advantaged students than for disadvantaged peers.

claim_text: Gates Millennium Scholars reduced financial strain and supported stronger persistence completion and STEM continuation outcomes.
notes: Open impact report emphasizes lower debt and work burdens plus stronger STEM and graduate-school pathways.

claim_text: Literacy-based promotion and retention policies can improve reading outcomes for struggling students but involve meaningful design and implementation tradeoffs.
notes: Treat this as a policy family with heterogeneous designs rather than one intervention.

claim_text: NCLB accountability provisions increased math achievement more clearly than reading achievement.
notes: Dee and Jacob report an effect size around 0.22 in grade 4 math by 2007 with no comparable reading gains.

claim_text: NCLB math gains were concentrated among students at the bottom of the achievement distribution and among subsidized-lunch students.
notes: This is one of the stronger equity-oriented findings in the national accountability literature.

claim_text: Chicago's high-stakes accountability policy increased high-stakes test scores without matching gains on a lower-stakes state exam.
notes: The study points to test-specific skill gains and effort effects rather than clean general learning gains.

claim_text: Chicago's accountability regime also produced strategic responses such as more special-education assignment preemptive retention and subject substitution away from science and social studies.
notes: Keep this visible so accountability effects are not presented as cost free.

claim_text: Chicago summer school remediation improved reading and math outcomes while retention effects differed by grade and subject.
notes: The third-grade results are more favorable than the sixth-grade math results.

claim_text: Florida's A+ accountability pressure improved outcomes more clearly in math than in reading for low-performing schools.
notes: The paper also argues that stigma and pressure explain more than voucher threat alone.

claim_text: Part of Florida A+ performance improvement appears test specific rather than a uniform learning gain across measures.
notes: Use this to support continuum views of accountability pressure versus durable learning.

claim_text: Career Academies increased long-run earnings for participants by about 11 percent on average with larger gains for young men.
notes: MDRC reports about 2088 dollars more per year overall and about 3731 dollars more per year for young men.

claim_text: Career Academies did not reduce high school completion or postsecondary participation while delivering labor-market benefits.
notes: This makes the model unusually strong among career-pathway interventions.

claim_text: P-TECH students were much more likely than control students to complete internships and to enroll in college-level courses during high school.
notes: MDRC reports a 38 percentage-point internship advantage and 46 versus 20 percent dual enrollment after four years.

claim_text: P-TECH students were more likely to complete an associate degree within seven years of entering high school.
notes: MDRC reports an approximately 5 percentage-point associate-degree advantage.

claim_text: CareerWise youth apprenticeships expand technical and employment pathways through paid work-based learning tied to employer demand.
notes: Current open evidence is stronger on pathway design and participant experience than on causal impact.

claim_text: The TX Sprouts school-garden intervention produced a statistically significant improvement in fourth-grade reading scores.
notes: The reported gain is about 6.5 percentage points with no broad claim warranted beyond the measured subgroup and subject.

claim_text: School-garden effects were mixed across grades and subjects with no significant fifth-grade reading or math gains in the TX Sprouts trial.
notes: Separate science-knowledge evidence supports the broader theme but not a blanket academic-gains claim.

claim_text: School cellphone bans improved student test scores after sustained implementation in high-use settings.
notes: The strongest gains appear in the second year and in middle and high schools.

claim_text: Phone bans also increased suspensions in the short run even as they reduced unexcused absences over time.
notes: This should be shown as a tradeoff rather than a clean win for low-tech restrictions.

claim_text: High-dosage tutoring is one of the strongest evidence-backed interventions for accelerating student learning after disruptions.
notes: Use this as a high-evidence contrast against lower-intensity academic recovery approaches.

claim_text: Scaling high-dosage tutoring requires sustained staffing and funding capacity and does not guarantee uniform implementation quality.
notes: Supports caution against assuming equal effects across all states and districts.

claim_text: AI in education currently has high federal policy attention but limited settled causal evidence on student outcome gains at national scale.
notes: Use as a high-tech policy track that requires ongoing evidence monitoring.

claim_text: Raise the Bar career pathways has elevated national attention to work-based learning and CTE-aligned pathway expansion.
notes: Treat as policy momentum plus implementation variation rather than one causal intervention.

claim_text: Evidence-based literacy priority promotes science-of-reading aligned practice but outcome gains depend heavily on implementation quality.
notes: Use as a literacy policy track linked to the broader SoR movement.
